ground_truth.title: What are the Phases and Timelines of a Home Renovation?
ground_truth.author: Block Renovation
ground_truth.main_content:
Renovating your space is an exciting endeavor that breathes new life into your home. However, embarking on a renovation project without a clear understanding of the different renovation phases and timelines can lead to frustration and delays. In this guide, we'll walk you through the core four renovation phases—planning, design, pre-construction, and construction—and shed light on the typical timelines for each stage.

Key factors that influence renovation timelines include project scope, permits and inspections, unexpected structural issues, contractor availability, weather conditions, and supply chain issues. More complex or extensive renovations that involve structural changes and electrical work will take much longer than simpler cosmetic refreshes.

The 15 granular phases of home renovation are: 1) Planning kickoff (1-2 weeks), 2) Budgeting and financing (1-2 weeks), 3) Contractor selection (1-2 weeks), 4) Design development (2-6 weeks), 5) Materials sourcing (1-3 months), 6) Pre-construction site surveys (1-2 weeks), 7) Permits and approvals (2 weeks to 3 months), 8) Demolition and pre-construction prep (1 week), 9) Structural and framing work (1-8 weeks), 10) Electrical, plumbing, and mechanical rough-in work (1-3 weeks), 11) Insulation and drywall installation (1-4 weeks), 12) Flooring, windows, and doors installation (1-2 weeks), 13) Cabinets, countertops, and fixtures installation (2-4 weeks), 14) Painting (1 week), and 15) Final inspection and site clean up (1 week).
with: The more you're able to capture the full picture of your renovation plan, the more accurate your cost and timeline estimates will be.
There are four overarching phases to every home renovation: planning, design, pre-construction, and construction.
Before construction breaks ground, it's wise to sort out where you'll be living during the construction phase.
Contractors make any adjustments to the structural framework of your home renovation site.
By allocating sufficient time for planning, design, pre-construction, and construction, you can minimize delays and unexpected setbacks.
